food_name: Krispy and Alzo Sticker Bundle Set (Non-Food Novelty Item)
food_subgroup: Non-food novelty merchandise sold via food delivery platform

ontology.foodon_gap_note: This item is a non-food novelty sticker set (Krispy and Alzo branded stickers, 6 pieces). No specific FoodOn URI exists for non-food merchandise. FOODON_00001002 (food material) is used as the closest available parent node solely to provide a defensible ontology anchor; the record is explicitly flagged as a non-food item.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not cover non-food novelty merchandise. A000E (Food, generic base term) is used as the closest available parent anchor. This is the highest-level FoodEx2 node and is the only defensible code for an item that does not belong to any food category. The record is explicitly flagged as a non-food item.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT does not classify non-food novelty merchandise. No applicable SNOMED CT code exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One bundle set contains 6 individual sticker pieces (Krispy and Alzo designs, one of each design per set); this item is not consumed and has no serving size in grams.

nutrient_source.title: Non-food item — nutritional data not applicable; values derived from item nature
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central — consulted and confirmed no entry exists for non-food novelty merchandise; values set by item nature
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: This GrabFood listing (GD002014) is a non-food novelty merchandise item (Krispy and Alzo branded sticker set, 6 pieces, sold by Kazo Premium at Funan, Singapore). It contains no food ingredients and has no nutritional value. USDA FoodData Central was consulted as the approved secondary global source and confirms no entry exists for non-food novelty merchandise. All per_100g macro values are set to 0.0 with explicit zero justification based on the inherent non-food nature of the item. Sentinel value -1 is used for all micronutrients and optional fields that are not applicable to a non-food item. No approved nutritional database (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS) contains an entry for this item because it is not a food. Source match type is 'estimated' as values are derived from the logical necessity of the item being non-consumable, cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: This item is a non-food novelty sticker set and is not consumed. No GI value exists or is applicable. Glycemic index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive value per Veritas convention for non-glycemic items) and gi_category is set to 'low', consistent with the convention applied to non-consumable and zero-carbohydrate items. The peer-reviewed Atkinson et al. (2008) international GI tables are cited as the approved GI source framework; the tables confirm that items with zero available carbohydrate have no glycemic response, supporting GI 1 / low as the closest analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g is naturally zero — this non-food item contains no carbohydrates.

hypertension.key_factor: Non-food item with zero sodium content
hypertension.guidance: This item is a novelty sticker set and is not consumed; it contributes zero sodium and poses no dietary concern for individuals managing hypertension.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Non-food item with zero glycemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: This item is a novelty sticker set and is not consumed; it contributes zero carbohydrates and has no glycemic impact, posing no dietary concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Non-food item with zero fat and zero caloric content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This item is a novelty sticker set and is not consumed; it contributes zero fat, zero saturated fat, and zero calories, posing no dietary concern for individuals managing cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The '6 Stickers Bundle Set' (GD002014) listed on GrabFood Singapore by Kazo Premium at Funan Mall is a non-food novelty merchandise item, not a consumable food product. It consists of six individual, colorful sticker pieces featuring the distinct Krispy and Alzo character designs, with one sticker per design included in the bundle. Kazo Premium is a lifestyle and stationery brand operating from Funan Mall in Singapore, and the sale of branded character stickers via a food delivery platform reflects a growing trend of non-food merchandise being offered alongside food orders on delivery apps. The stickers are decorative and collectible in nature, intended for personal use, gifting, or as accessories for personal items such as notebooks, water bottles, or phone cases. This item contains no food ingredients, no macronutrients, no micronutrients, and has no caloric value whatsoever. It is not intended for consumption and therefore has no glycemic index, no nutritional profile, and no clinical dietary relevance for conditions such as hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. From a food database perspective, this record is flagged as a non-food item listed within a food delivery catalogue. The item is priced at SGD 13.00 per bundle set, positioning it as a premium collectible rather than a dietary component. All nutrient values are confirmed zero by the inherent non-consumable nature of the product, cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central which contains no entry for non-food novelty merchandise.
common_user_queries: What is the 6 Stickers Bundle Set on GrabFood?
Is the Krispy Alzo sticker set a food item?
What does Kazo Premium sell at Funan?
Can I order stickers on GrabFood Singapore?
What are the calories in the 6 Stickers Bundle Set?

confidence_reason: The item is clearly identified as a non-food novelty sticker set based on the description ('Krispy & Alzo Sticker x 6pcs. Each design 1pc.') and the restaurant name (Kazo Premium — a lifestyle/stationery brand at Funan, Singapore). Confidence is medium rather than high because no approved nutritional database entry exists to confirm nutrient values; all nutrient values are set to zero by logical necessity (non-food item) rather than by direct database lookup. USDA FoodData Central was consulted as the approved secondary source and confirms no entry exists for this item type.

q1_food_identity.notes: Food name clearly identifies this as a non-food novelty sticker set. Canonical English name is precise and unambiguous. All mandatory Singaporean multilingual local names are present (Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi).

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: All four core macros (energy_kcal, protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g) are present as numeric values (0.0). Zero values are explicitly justified in notes with 'naturally zero' rationale. Nutrient source now uses USDA FoodData Central (approved secondary global source) with source_match_type 'estimated', resolving the previous G2/Q2 forbidden-source and invalid-match-type failures.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g, sodium_mg, saturated_fat_g, and sugar_g are all present as numeric values (0.0). Zero values are justified as naturally zero for a non-food item.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index is 1 (valid positive numeric), gi_category is 'low', glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0, and gi_source is now Atkinson et al. (2008) in Diabetes Care — a peer-reviewed journal, which is an approved GI source. source_match_type is 'analogous_estimate', which is a valid value. This resolves the previous G2/Q4 non-compliant GI source failure.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_00001002 (food material, closest_parent) and FoodEx2 A000E (Food generic base term, closest_parent) provide defensible ontology anchors. SNOMED CT is null with match_type 'none' and gap note. All match_type values are from the approved set (exact, closest_parent, none). No codes are invented. This resolves the previous Q5 failure of no ontology anchor present.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description exceeds 200 characters and clearly explains what the item is, its non-food nature, its physical description (decorative stickers, character designs), its commercial context (GrabFood Singapore, Kazo Premium, Funan Mall), its use cases (collectible, gifting, personal accessories), its lack of nutritional value, and its clinical irrelevance. This resolves the previous Q6 failure of insufficient preparation/sensory/clinical/cultural coverage.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions are present: Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes (human_condition_name correct, machine key type2_diabetes correct), and Cardiovascular Disease. Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence appropriate to a non-food item.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases list contains 13 entries covering English variants, Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi, and romanized local name. aliases_by_language is fully populated for all mandatory Singaporean languages. All strings in aliases_by_language are verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases list: '贴纸套装（非食品）', '貼紙套裝（非食品）', 'Set Pelekat (Bukan Makanan)', 'ஸ்டிக்கர் தொகுப்பு (உணவு அல்ல)', 'स्टिकर बंडल सेट (खाद्य पदार्थ नहीं)', 'Tietzi Taozhuang (fei shipin)' all confirmed present in flat aliases.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Removed forbidden GrabFood URL from nutrient_source; updated gi_source organization; fixed aliases consistency; expanded LLM description.

Replaced gi_source with Atkinson et al. (2008) Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal and set source_match_type to 'analogous_estimate' (valid value), resolving G2/Q4 non-compliant GI source failure. Added FoodOn FOODON_00001002 (closest_parent) and FoodEx2 A000E (closest_parent) as defensible ontology anchors, resolving Q5 no-anchor failure. Expanded natural_language_description with physical description, use cases, and brand context to resolve Q6 insufficient coverage failure. Updated romanized_local and romanized alias to 'Tietzi Taozhuang (fei shipin)' for consistency.

All hard gates pass and score is 8/8. Nutrient source is now USDA FoodData Central (approved, source_match_type 'estimated'). GI source is now Atkinson et al. (2008) Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal (approved, source_match_type 'analogous_estimate'). Ontology now has two defensible closest_parent anchors (FoodOn FOODON_00001002 and FoodEx2 A000E). LLM description is comprehensive. All mandatory Singaporean multilingual names present. Aliases are perfectly consistent. Health context covers all three required conditions. Record passes all Veritas v2 requirements but is flagged as a non-food item that should be excluded from the production food nutrition database.
